commands.review_summary:
The PR seeds bundled channel runtime setters from loaded plugin runtime before gateway account startup, persists channel origin/runtime metadata in the plugin registry, and adds Feishu, gateway, and registry regression tests.

PR surface: Source +63, Tests +122. Total +185 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main shows Feishu handlers depend on `getFeishuRuntime()` while monitor startup does not seed it, and the linked reports provide matching versioned logs. I did not run a live Feishu WebSocket reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer proof is needed rather than an automated code repair; the linked issue should remain open until this PR merges or is replaced.

target.title: Fix Feishu monitor runtime initialization
target.body:
Fixes #88024

Note: this pull request was made with AI assistance.

## Summary
- Seed bundled channel runtime setters with the full scoped plugin runtime before account startup.
- Persist channel `origin` alongside runtime registrations in the real `createPluginRegistry` channel path, so bundled Feishu entries are detected in production registry state.
- Add gateway, registry, and Feishu startup regression coverage for runtime seeding.

## Verification
- `docker run --rm -v /tmp/openclaw-feishu-fix:/workspace -v /tmp/openclaw-work.DNxJRE/repo/node_modules:/workspace/node_modules -w /workspace node:24-bookworm bash -lc 'corepack enable && OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts'` (43 tests passed)

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Feishu WebSocket monitor startup now seeds the bundled channel runtime setter from the loaded plugin runtime before account startup, so inbound handlers using `getFeishuRuntime()` do not see an uninitialized runtime.

Real environment tested: Containerized OpenClaw checkout using `node:24-bookworm` with workspace dependencies installed; no live Feishu credentials.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `docker run --rm -v /tmp/openclaw-feishu-fix:/workspace -v /tmp/openclaw-work.DNxJRE/repo/node_modules:/workspace/node_modules -w /workspace node:24-bookworm bash -lc 'corepack enable && OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts'`

Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the container run:
```console
RUN v4.1.7 /workspace
✓ extension-feishu ../../extensions/feishu/src/monitor.startup.test.ts (6 tests) 6646ms
✓ plugins ../../src/plugins/registry.runtime-config.test.ts (3 tests) 36ms
✓ gateway-server ../../src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts (34 tests) 27ms
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 43 passed (43)
```

Observed result after fix: The registry test proves the real `api.registerChannel` path stores `origin: "bundled"` with the scoped runtime registration, and the gateway test proves bundled runtime setters receive the full plugin runtime before startup.

What was not tested: Live Feishu WebSocket delivery with real Feishu app credentials.
